scope: Full-step critical-path interpretation for the retained TorchTitan DeepSeek-V4 Flash 4-layer 8xMI350 target canary.
how_to_read: These buckets are not additive wall-clock decomposition. Torch profiler annotation ranges, CUDA event sidecars, and kernel-family summaries have different synchronization boundaries.
Use the no-profile CE2 run for headline throughput and use profiled runs for relative target ranking.
A large label is not automatically a high-return optimization target. If the label mostly contains rank waiting, the useful lever is overlap or skew reduction, not only the kernel named on the stack.

holistic_ranked_ladder.bucket: MoE backward dX return skew and overlap
holistic_ranked_ladder.interpretation: This is a MoE backward critical-path issue, but mostly peer-arrival skew hidden inside combine rather than raw combine bandwidth.
highest_return_actions: Make dX return overlap real by starting return earlier or in chunks.
Reduce owner-rank VJP imbalance that feeds the combine wait.
Revisit AsyncLL only with shared route/order metadata or explicit per-dispatched-row route identity.
deprioritized_actions: Blind MORI [block, rdma, warp] tuple sweeps.
Independent-handle AsyncLL throughput A/Bs before seeded route/order parity passes.

holistic_ranked_ladder.bucket: Routed MoE owner-rank VJP GEMM family
holistic_ranked_ladder.interpretation: This is real MoE math and it also feeds the combine skew. EP8 smooths local VJP versus EP4, but the six padded/recompute GEMM groups are still a structural wall.
highest_return_actions: Replace padded owner-rank VJP with fused/grouped training GEMM for w13 forward, w2 forward/dgrad, w2/w13 wgrad, and xgrad.
Change the AITER/MORI forward-backward contract to expose gate/up/hidden and compact output, or fuse recompute inside backward.
Keep Triton SwiGLU as retained vector fusion, but do not spend the next pass on pure SwiGLU alone.

holistic_ranked_ladder.interpretation: The loss/lm-head path is a real canary lever and CE2 is retained. Its relative size is amplified in this 4-layer canary, so it should not displace MoE and sparse attention as the final full-model kernel priorities.
highest_return_actions: Keep CE2 as the exact 8x S4096 GBS128 launcher default.
Only revisit loss after the MoE/attention path changes or if full-model profiling still shows it as a large exclusive bucket.

holistic_ranked_ladder.interpretation: Attention is not the largest current guarded-path bucket, but the final sparse MLA/LI fused backward remains mandatory because the current canary is still a guarded/prototype sparse path.
highest_return_actions: Continue the compressed dKV many-to-one accumulation work as the final sparse-MLA/LI blocker.
Avoid dense route materialization and group-major row-wise recompute; preserve query-major reuse and reduce dKV writes in-kernel.

holistic_ranked_ladder.interpretation: Communication and movement are material but not yet the dominant single-node target. FSDP annotation ranges can include overlap and should be interpreted separately from kernel-family NCCL totals.
highest_return_actions: Keep FSDP and NCCL tags in future profiles.
Re-rank after MoE overlap and final sparse attention changes alter overlap.

holistic_ranked_ladder.bucket: Shared expert dense backward scope check
holistic_ranked_ladder.interpretation: A broad trace can label shared expert backward around 1.9-2.0 s/rank/step, but the deeper dense/loss sidecar puts owner-tagged shared expert linears near 0.05 s/step. Treat the broad number as non-exclusive scope bleed until reproduced by an exclusive GPU-event probe.
highest_return_actions: Keep shared expert labels in future profiles.
Do not prioritize shared expert dense tuning ahead of MoE overlap/VJP, loss CE, or final sparse attention without stronger exclusive evidence.

rejected_or_low_return_branches.evidence: S128 smokes pass, but S4096 route_key and src_pos alignment both fail because independent handles do not share a production-safe row order or route identity.
EP8 pure SwiGLU forward+backward is about 0.104 s/rank/step, far below MoE GEMM and combine.
Layer-2 dense-route BMM/flat GEMM are correct but around 23-25 ms with multi-GiB peak memory, so they are not the final sparse-attention backward path.

next_profile_contract: For headline throughput, run no-profiler 8x S4096 GBS128 first and compare tok/GPU/s and step time.
For attribution, keep MORI CUDA-event sidecar, dense-linear owner tags, attention kernel family tags, FSDP/NCCL tags, and loss chunk tags in the same 2-step profile.
After any MoE overlap change, report both local VJP duration and combine wait/skew; do not report combine alone as raw communication bandwidth.
After any sparse attention change, report dQ, dK/dV, compressed dKV accumulation, LI/compressor gradients, memory peak, and whether selected-state guard is still active.
bottom_line: The current retained path already passes the normalized 8xMI350 canary target, but the highest-return path toward a production DeepSeek-V4 Flash training stack is MoE backward overlap/skew plus fused owner-rank VJP, followed by the retained CE2 loss setting and final sparse MLA/LI fused backward work.
